_comment: Shared GIBBERISH-gate PARITY vectors (companion to junk_gate_vectors.json). The Dart client gate (capecho_capture_core isLikelyGibberish, gibberish_filter_test.dart) AND the TS twin (backend src/input.ts isLikelyGibberish, input.test.ts) BOTH assert against these so the two hand-ported implementations can't silently diverge. 'pass' = isLikelyGibberish MUST be false (it reaches /explain). It includes: real words (the silent-failure guard); same-row words proving a direction flip resets the walk (were/tree); liberty/property/poverty/puberty/dirty/thirty/overtype which CONTAIN a keyboard run ('erty') but are not a WHOLE-token walk (the substring form false-rejected ~47 dictionary words, dual CR); AND bare 4-key walks (asdf/qwer/hjkl/zxcv/lkjh/mnbv) plus the lone 4-key real word 'wert' (archaic 'thou wert') which the >=5-key floor leaves to the L3 model gate, keeping L1 PROVABLY free of real-word false-rejects (313k-word brute-force). 'reject' = isLikelyGibberish MUST be true: the WHOLE token is a monotonic >=5-key single-row keyboard walk, OR a >=4 identical-character repeat. NOTE: the test ALSO asserts every junk_gate_vectors 'pass' word is non-gibberish. Phase D2 adds zh rows: representative word classes (多音字 行/银行, 成语 一石二鸟, 离合词 见面, AABB reduplication 高高兴兴/马马虎虎) MUST pass — the keyboard-walk signal is Latin-only by construction and the >=4 identical-char repeat is the only script-agnostic signal, so 哈哈哈哈 (a held-key/expressive repeat, not vocabulary) rejects while no real zh word does (AABB repeats alternate characters).
